claim: Crawl4AI converts website HTML into clean markdown specifically formatted for LLM consumption, replacing raw messy HTML with human-readable text that LLMs can understand far better.
actionable: Use Crawl4AI instead of raw HTML scraping: `pip install crawl4ai && crawl4ai.setup()` then invoke with `AsyncWebCrawler` to get markdown-formatted web content for your knowledge base.
source_title: Turn ANY Website into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Crawl4AI handles proxies, session management, JavaScript rendering (via Playwright), and irrelevant content removal automatically—solving problems that are hard to implement from scratch.
actionable: Leverage Crawl4AI's built-in Playwright integration for dynamic sites; don't try to replicate its proxy and session management with basic HTTP requests.
source_title: Turn ANY Website into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Extract all URLs from a website via its sitemap.xml (append /sitemap.xml to homepage), then use Crawl4AI's multi-URL crawling with batch processing to scrape hundreds of pages efficiently.
actionable: Parse sitemap.xml to get all page URLs; use Crawl4AI's batch crawling with batch_size=10 to visit multiple pages in parallel, drastically faster than sequential crawling.
source_title: Turn ANY Website into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Crawl4AI batch processing uses a single browser session across parallel URL fetches, keeping memory usage ultra-low (119 MB for 42 pages) compared to sequential crawling.
actionable: Use batch crawling mode with one browser session and multiple concurrent URLs rather than spawning a new browser per page, reducing memory footprint by 90%+.
source_title: Turn ANY Website into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Before scraping any website, check robots.txt and respect its crawl rules—platforms like GitHub request explicit permission before crawling.
actionable: Always check `<domain>/robots.txt` before scraping; if it says 'contact us first', reach out for permission to avoid ethical and legal issues.
source_title: Turn ANY Website into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: Crawl4AI markdown output is ideal for RAG knowledge bases—feed it to a vector database to create LLM-expert systems without manual curation.
actionable: Build RAG pipelines: Crawl4AI → markdown → vector DB (pgvector, Pinecone) → retrieval-augmented LLM queries for domain-specific AI agents.
source_title: Turn ANY Website into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS

claim: A Pydantic AI RAG agent trained on framework docs via Crawl4AI can answer questions and link to source pages, making the agent credible and auditable.
actionable: After crawling docs into a vector DB, build an agent that retrieves relevant passages and includes source citations so users can verify answers.
source_title: Turn ANY Website into LLM Knowledge in SECONDS
